content.problemStatement:
Let SSS be a set of NNN boolean variables X1,X2,…,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1​,X2​,…,XN​ and their negations ¬X1,¬X2,…,¬XN\neg X_1, \neg X_2, \ldots, \neg X_N¬X1​,¬X2​,…,¬XN​. You are given the initial values of all variables.

A 2-CNF boolean expression is defined as a conjunction of clauses, where each clause is a disjunction of two elements of the set SSS. Ada constructed a 2-CNF boolean expression BBB with MMM clauses as follows:


First, Ada painted each of the variables X1,X2,…,XNX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_NX1​,X2​,…,XN​ with one colour ― either red or black.
Then, she painted the negated variables ― for each valid iii, if XiX_iXi​ is painted red, then ¬Xi\neg X_i¬Xi​ is painted black, and vice versa (if XiX_iXi​ is black, ¬Xi\neg X_i¬Xi​ is painted red).
Finally, she wrote the boolean expression B=(P1∨Q1)∧(P2∨Q2)∧…∧(PM∨QM)B = (P_1 \lor Q_1) \wedge (P_2 \lor Q_2) \wedge \ldots \wedge (P_M \lor Q_M)B=(P1​∨Q1​)∧(P2​∨Q2​)∧…∧(PM​∨QM​), in such a way that no clause contains two variables with the same colour and Pi≠¬QiP_i \neq \neg Q_iPi​=¬Qi​ for each clause.
Ada wants the expression to evaluate to "true". In order to achieve that, she may change the values of variables; for each valid iii, the cost of changing the value of XiX_iXi​ (from "true" to "false" or vice versa) is CiC_iCi​. Find the minimum total cost needed to make the expression BBB evaluate to "true" or determine that it is impossible.

Note that you are not given the colours of variables, but it is guaranteed that the expression BBB is chosen in such a way that there is at least one valid way to assign colours and the minimum cost is the same for each valid assignment of colours.
constraints: each character of SSS is either '0' or '1'
the sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 43,21043,21043,210
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains a binary string SSS with length NNN describing the initial values of the variables. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of SSS is '1' if XiX_iXi​ is true or '0' if XiX_iXi​ is false.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​.
MMM lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers PiP_iPi​ and QiQ_iQi​. For each valid iii, the variable XiX_iXi​ is represented by the integer iii and its negation is represented by −i-i−i.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum cost needed to make the boolean expression evaluate to "true", or −1-1−1 if it is impossible.

One optimal way to make the expression true is changing the value of X3X_3X3​.
